article_meta.title: — News —Study Shows Pandemic Saw Rise In Firearm Injuries Among KidsBy Charlotte Pence Bond•Dec 20, 2022 DailyWire.com•F

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains some arguable author-level bias instances. The phrase 'another disastrous effect of the pandemic on kids' is the author's own characterization using the loaded word 'disastrous' — this is a Subjective Qualifying Adjective or Word Choice bias. The phrase 'a spike in gun injuries' uses 'spike' which is mildly sensational but more defensible as common journalistic shorthand. The phrase 'much higher' in 'was also much higher during the period of the pandemic' is a vague intensifier in author prose. The overall framing leans toward emphasizing negative pandemic effects without balancing perspectives (potential Slant). However, the article is largely a straightforward reporting of study findings with proper attribution, so the bias instances are relatively mild. The most clear-cut miss is 'disastrous effect.' The model did correctly avoid flagging any quoted text, which is good. Overall this is a moderate false negative — not as severe as returning [] for an article with multiple obviously loaded phrases throughout.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations — nothing was flagged at all, let alone quoted text.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so no false positives exist. Score 1.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed at least one clear instance: 'another disastrous effect of the pandemic on kids' is the author's own loaded characterization. There's also arguably 'a spike in gun injuries' and the overall framing/slant. Missing 'disastrous' is a moderate false negative but the article is predominantly neutral reporting.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No items were flagged so no type labels to evaluate. Score 1 by default.
